title: Resource
description:
A Resource is an Entity the organization manages — a thing the system tracks, mutates, or operates on. Loans, Accounts, Products, Documents, Invoices.

Resources are one of four noun primitives (alongside Person, Role, and Group); all four share the noun base shape: name, optional attributes[], optional actions[], optional parent. People-specific concerns (scope, memberships, eligibility) live on Person/Role/Group/Membership, not on Resource.

name.description: The singular PascalCase name of the Resource.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of what this Resource represents in the domain.

domain.description: The dot-separated domain path this Resource belongs to.

actions.description: Verbs this Resource supports — the catalog of what can be done to it.

parent.description: Optional parent Resource name for hierarchical relationships (e.g., LineItem.parent = Invoice).

examples.description: Representative example records for this Resource. Used as stub data when the API is unavailable.

examples.name: Loan
examples.description: A financial loan issued to a borrower with repayment terms.
